pi_items.source: Medical literature: Tachycardia threshold = HR > 100 bpm. Class 2 hemorrhagic shock: 100-120 BPM. Task description: 'approximately 110 bpm (simulating tachycardia)'
Medical literature: Class 2 hemorrhagic shock vital signs typically 100-120 BPM. Task description: 'approximately 110 bpm (simulating tachycardia)'
Medical literature: Hemodynamic instability defined as systolic BP < 90 mmHg. Task description: 'approximately 90 mmHg (simulating hypotension)'
Medical literature: Class 2 shock hypotension ranges ~80-100 systolic. Task description: 'approximately 90 mmHg (simulating hypotension)'
Task description line: 'Infusion Rate to any value > 10 mL/hr' explicitly states GREATER THAN 10. Metadata specifies min=10, which includes 10 itself. Discrepancy between task and metadata.

Task description: 'Write a status note to `/home/ga/Desktop/shock_protocol_status.txt`'. setup_task.sh line 35: 'rm -f /home/ga/Desktop/shock_protocol_status.txt'. export_result.sh line 56: 'NOTE_FILE=/home/ga/Desktop/shock_protocol_status.txt'

Task uses OpenICE as per task.json env_id field and all scripts. Web search confirms OpenICE is a real, open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by MD PnP Program under NIH/NIBIB funding. Implements ASTM F2761 standard for Integrated Clinical Environment.

pi_items.metadata_value: Multiparameter Monitor, NIBP device, Infusion Pump
pi_items.verified_value: Three simulated medical devices as described
pi_items.source: Task description explicitly requires: (1) Multiparameter Monitor for heart rate, (2) Non-Invasive Blood Pressure (NIBP) device, (3) Infusion Pump. export_result.sh lines 31-42 check for these devices in logs using keywords: 'Multiparameter|MultiParam', 'NIBP|NonInvasive|BloodPressure', 'Infusion|Pump|IcePump'

pi_items.verified_value: Vital Signs clinical application to be launched
pi_items.source: Task description line 4: 'Launch the Vital Signs clinical application to view the integrated data.' export_result.sh line 47: checks for 'VitalSigns|Vital Signs|ClinicalApp' in logs

pi_items.verified_value: Hemodynamic instability parameters: Tachycardia (HR ~110), Hypotension (SBP ~90), Vasopressor support (>10 mL/hr)
pi_items.source: Medical literature confirms: (1) HR > 100 = tachycardia sign of instability; (2) SBP < 90 = hypotension indicative of shock; (3) Shock index (HR/SBP) ≥0.6 indicates clinical shock (110/90 = 1.22 >> 0.6). These parameters collectively represent Class 2 hemorrhagic shock or similar hemodynamic instability state requiring vasopressor support.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic medical simulation task using OpenICE (a real, open-source platform for medical device interoperability developed under NIH/NIBIB funding). The task requires configuring three simulated medical devices to demonstrate hemodynamic instability: (1) Multiparameter Monitor set to ~110 bpm heart rate (tachycardia threshold), (2) Non-Invasive Blood Pressure device set to ~90 mmHg systolic (hypotension threshold), and (3) Infusion Pump set >10 mL/hr (simulating vasopressor support). These parameter ranges (HR 105-115, BP 85-95, pump >10 mL/hr) are clinically accurate for representing Class 2 hemorrhagic shock or hemodynamic instability. One discrepancy exists: the task description specifies pump rate >10 mL/hr but metadata minimum is 10 (inclusive), creating a boundary condition inconsistency. All other parameters are verified against medical literature and task specifications.
